Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties David & Trudy Prescott
Jordan B. Everakes of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant Duro Dyne Corporation

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(a) and (d). Trial is May 20, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is May 16, 2013. The applicable C.C.P. shall apply to all summary motions briefing schedules. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than April 26, 2013. Defendants to respond no later than May 3. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on May 9, 2013. Time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 3 , 2013. Expert discovery cut off is May 17, 2013. No later than Feb. 21, 2013, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than Feb, 21, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 1, 2013 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference on May 17, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. with the Court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court 5. Parties are required to submit statements by May 15, at 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff's and defendants' representatives fully knowledgeable about the case and with authorities to settle must be present, unless the Court determines, prior to the settlement conference, that good cause exists to have that person/persons appear by telephone. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 regarding the submission of settlement conference statements. The statements will be kept confidential. All parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
